notes: agy's REPL buffers mid-turn input; the filtered transcript.jsonl is DONE-only and writes no turn boundary for queued text, so the parser sees one contiguous working span (no →ready flicker when the follow-up lands). The cycle ends at ready on the final empty terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE. EMISSION CAVEAT: no sample exercises queueing — record must confirm the single contiguous working span. Single-birth adapter; tokens/cost absent (SQLite-only).

text: First user prompt drives ready → working
A second prompt is typed into the TUI mid-turn and buffered. State stays working — no flicker to ready when the queued input lands, no flicker to waiting
The cycle eventually ends at ready (final empty terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE) — whether the queued message folds into the turn or is consumed as a follow-up, both shapes finish ready
